statement: The system shall allow each team to manage its own budget while the IFA can control and monitor the overall balance to ensure proper management.
The system shall allow specification of budget policies and generate alerts for policy violations.
The system shall allow the financial team to access financial data of teams and clubs.
The system shall allow administrative users to view diverse transactions.
The system shall allow referees to insert game events in real time via a mobile phone.
The system shall allow unregistered guests to view final game result data.
The system shall allow users to export data to journalists or press.
The system shall allow team managers and team administrators to view their team's data and perform offline analysis.
The system shall allow only teams to insert budget transactions and access their own data, while allowing the IFA to view all monetary information and request details without modifying the data.
The system shall allow definition of scheduling policies.
The system shall allocate referees to games based on defined policies and prevent a referee from officiating multiple games involving the same teams.
The system shall enable referees to set their preferences and constraints, such as vacation days, which are considered in scheduling.
The system shall allow manual adjustment of the schedule.
The system shall require an explanation and approval by a second IFA representative for any schedule override.
The system shall require approval from two IFA reviewers for any schedule change.
The system shall enforce a minimum gap of at least two days between matches for the same team.
The system shall allow fans to subscribe to specific games or players and receive push notifications.

The system shall provide three sections in the fan portal: a follow teams/players section with notifications, a live updates from referees section, and a live scores section, and shall allow users to subscribe to information and receive notifications.
The system shall provide a web‑based application for teams and IFA administration and mobile applications for referees and fans.
The system shall provide a unified system with distinct interfaces for each stakeholder role.
The system shall permit only official referees to update game data.
The system shall send automatic reminder notifications for periodic reports and remind users five minutes before a game starts.
The system shall support up to 50,000 concurrent users.
The system shall provide a response time of one to two seconds for fans and administrators, and prioritize real‑time responsiveness for referees.
The system shall encrypt budget data to prevent leakage.
The system shall allow players to update their social network page within the portal.
The system shall allow a team manager to submit a registration request with documents, have the IFA approve it, and then enable the team to manage its players, coaches, stadium, and resources.
The system shall retain all past scheduling archives.
The system shall allow the IFA to define leagues, define seasons, appoint or register referees, and set budget and scheduling policies.
The system shall deliver all notifications within the application or website and shall not send notifications via email or direct messages.
